_comment: Example nanobot config wiring Dormy MCP. Two modes shown — pick one for your ~/.nanobot/config.json. Existing keys (agents, channels, providers, gateway, api, etc.) live alongside; this file shows only what's new. See docs/nanobot-integration.md for full architecture.

mcp_servers._pick_one: Mode A (stdio, local dev) OR Mode B (HTTP, production)

dormy_modeA_stdio._comment: Nanobot spawns dormy-ai as a stdio subprocess. Use for local dev / testing nanobot+Telegram against your local code. Replace the path with your actual dormy-ai checkout location.

dormy_modeB_http._comment: Production: nanobot connects to deployed Dormy MCP at mcp.heydormy.ai over HTTPS. Authorization header carries the user's OpenRouter key (BYOK). For v0.1 'dormy_router' mode, the same shared key is used for every chat — see docs/nanobot-integration.md.

defaults._comment: Disable nanobot's built-in Dream long-term memory — Dormy uses user_observations table (see PR #1). Otherwise Dream files (~/.nanobot/workspace/{SOUL,USER,MEMORY}.md) leak across founders in multi-tenant SaaS.

telegram._comment: Replace token after revoking the spike token (8696904581:AAH...). Production should register @HeyDormyBot or similar via BotFather, store the new token in Railway env. allowFrom: ['*'] = SaaS multi-user mode (per-user session isolation handled by nanobot's channel × chat_id keying).
